rules: [P1] When Immunization search returns empty, try alternative coding systems (e.g., CVX, SNOMED) or text search.

[P1] For imaging procedures, use ImagingStudy resource with a standard SNOMED or LOINC code (e.g., CT Abdomen) instead of local or non-standard codes; if no results, retry with modality code or text search before exploring other resources.
[P1] When checking lab results, retrieve both required values (e.g., TSH and free T4) before applying protocol rules.
[P1] When a search returns zero results, first verify patient identifier and resource type before removing filters or concluding absence.
[P1] Ensure date values follow FHIR format (YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ) when using date range modifiers.
[P1] For procedure searches, try alternative coding systems (SNOMED, CPT) if initial codes yield no results.
[P1] When codes yield no results, use text search with common procedure names before concluding absence.
[P1] Always confirm patient identity and record accessibility before any resource query.
[P1] Use LOINC or CVX codes for vaccine queries (e.g., COVID-19 vaccine codes) rather than relying on resource type alone.
[P1] Always validate FHIR search parameters (e.g., date format, parameter names) before making a request.
[P1] Check date range logic: ensure date=ge uses correct threshold relative to current date.
[P1] For vaccination history, always query the Immunization resource with date range filters (not Procedure or MedicationRequest alone).

[P1] Before concluding absence of active medications, search the MedicationRequest resource with status=active and use multiple standard coding systems (e.g., RxNorm, SNOMED, NDC) for all relevant drug classes.
[P1] Avoid repeated searches without advancing towards the final goal.
[P1] When ordering a vaccine, use CVX codes (not CPT codes) in the medicationCodeableConcept and verify the code system is http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/cvx.
[P1] To ensure completeness of observations over a time range, query with appropriate time filters and broaden if initial results are few.
[P1] Do not search for conditions as a substitute for procedures unless explicitly instructed.
[P1] Apply threshold rules (e.g., TSH >10) only after confirming all relevant data has been searched.
[P1] If no matching procedure exists, conclude no action is required and finish.
[P1] Do not proceed to order a new study until confirming no existing relevant records through thorough search.
[P1] If first search returns empty, check alternative FHIR resources before retrying same pattern.

[P1] When no prior record exists for a time-threshold condition, explicitly check the threshold (e.g., 365 days) rather than assuming absence of any record, and treat the event as having occurred before the threshold if no record is found within that window.
[P1] If a required lab value is absent, explicitly document the missing data before applying protocol rules.
[P1] If initial search returns no results, broaden the time window or try alternative codes before concluding absence.
[P1] After ordering, confirm the order details (e.g., vaccine type, route, date) before finalizing.
